quotes.quote: this process is tightly integrated with a literature review module that queries external academic databases to identify knowledge gaps and avoid rediscovering existing work
quotes.rationale: Prior-art corpus queried to judge novelty of proposals.

quotes.quote: reflection rounds to refine proposals based on the literature search results
quotes.rationale: Output routed to revision of candidate proposals, not a terminal gate.

quotes.quote: At the end of each stage, an LLM-based evaluator assesses all leaf nodes and selects the most promising one to serve as the root for the next stage of exploration, thus effectively pruning less promising research avenues.
quotes.rationale: LLM judgment directly governs stage transition and pruning; closed computational feedback.

quotes.quote: the plots generated are fed to a VLM, which is prompted to act as a scientist and critique them
quotes.rationale: VLM critiques generated artifacts during experimentation.

quotes.quote: This feedback is used to generate new experimental nodes in the tree search aimed at addressing the identified issues.
quotes.rationale: Feedback routed into new nodes, i.e. candidate revision and search expansion.

quotes.quote: When choosing between non-buggy nodes, the system uses a best-first search strategy guided by GPT-4o, which evaluates candidates based on factors like performance metrics, training dynamics and the quality of the plots generated.
quotes.rationale: LLM-guided evaluation controls which node is expanded, i.e. search control.

quotes.quote: The selected nodes are expanded by creating a new child node.
quotes.rationale: Confirms policy-control routing within the search.

quotes.quote: The agent processes the PDF of a manuscript to produce a structured review, including numerical scores for soundness, presentation and contribution, along with a list of strengths and weaknesses and a preliminary accept or reject decision
quotes.rationale: Terminal manuscript-level assessment with scores and accept/reject.

quotes.quote: The Automated Reviewer achieved a comparable balanced accuracy with humans (69% versus 66%; see Supplementary Information section A.3.2 for details) and a higher F1 score compared with inter-human group agreement (0.62 versus 0.49)
quotes.rationale: Discrimination against human decisions plus inter-reviewer agreement comparison; not calibration.

quotes.quote: We manually filtered the most promising outputs at each stage (Supplementary Information section A.4).
quotes.rationale: Human selection of outputs for submission.

quotes.quote: This process resulted in three complete manuscripts being selected for submission.
quotes.rationale: Selection is terminal for submission; no reported routing back into the search.

quotes.quote: The entire scientific workflow for each paper, from ideation and coding to manuscript writing, was performed without any human modification.
quotes.rationale: Human authority is select-only, not modification of content or execution.

quotes.quote: These three submissions were included among the 43 papers reviewed for the workshop. Reviewers were informed that some of the submissions were AI-generated but not which ones, ensuring a blind process.
quotes.rationale: Blind external human review process outside the system; no reported feedback into the system.

quotes.quote: At present, The AI Scientist conducts computational experiments only.
quotes.rationale: Establishes current fidelity ceiling: no physical assay validation channel.

quotes.quote: In future work, this same playbook could be applied to other scientific domains where one can automatically conduct experiments (or have humans conduct them)
quotes.rationale: Physical-domain extension is proposed but not exercised.

policy_type: LLM-guided best-first tree search over experimental nodes
LLM leaf-node evaluation for stage transition and pruning
VLM plot critique spawning new experimental nodes
literature-grounded idea generation with reflection rounds
human manual filtering of outputs at each stage
memory_structure: experiment tree of nodes with parent-child expansion and stage roots
replication nodes with aggregation nodes summarizing prior results
literature review results from external academic databases

diagnostics.statement: The LLM evaluator that selects the leaf node serving as root for the next experimental stage has no reported reliability study for that selection target.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: At the end of each stage, an LLM-based evaluator assesses all leaf nodes and selects the most promising one to serve as the root for the next stage of exploration, thus effectively pruning less promising research avenues.

diagnostics.consequence: An unvalidated LLM judgment irreversibly prunes research avenues, so systematic evaluator error would silently eliminate viable lines with no downstream detection.

diagnostics.statement: The Automated Reviewer's benchmark evidence is discrimination and agreement against human decisions on an aggregate accept/reject target; it is not property-specific evidence for novelty and not a probabilistic calibration analysis.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: a preliminary accept or reject decision

The Automated Reviewer achieved a comparable balanced accuracy with humans (69% versus 66%; see Supplementary Information section A.3.2 for details) and a higher F1 score compared with inter-human group agreement (0.62 versus 0.49)

diagnostics.consequence: Reviewer benchmark results cannot be transferred to the ideation novelty filter or to score calibration claims without overstating validated reliability.

diagnostics.statement: The Automated Reviewer output is reported only as a terminal manuscript assessment; the packet reports no routing of its scores into node selection, stage transition, or any tree-search action.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The agent processes the PDF of a manuscript to produce a structured review
selects the most promising one to serve as the root for the next stage of exploration

diagnostics.consequence: Closure for the manuscript-quality target is open: quality assessment cannot correct earlier search decisions, so poor papers are detected only after production.

diagnostics.statement: External blind workshop review is reported as an outside assessment with no reported channel returning those reviews to internal search or memory.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: These three submissions were included among the 43 papers reviewed for the workshop. Reviewers were informed that some of the submissions were AI-generated but not which ones, ensuring a blind process.

diagnostics.consequence: The only independent human evidence about output quality is external-only, leaving the loop open and the system unable to learn from independent judgment.

diagnostics.statement: Human manual filtering of the most promising outputs at each stage sits between the autonomous workflow and the reported submissions, while the workflow itself is stated to be unmodified.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: We manually filtered the most promising outputs at each stage (Supplementary Information section A.4).
The entire scientific workflow for each paper, from ideation and coding to manuscript writing, was performed without any human modification.

diagnostics.consequence: The system boundary for autonomy claims is ambiguous: selection authority is human while generation authority is not, and readers may conflate the two.

diagnostics.statement: All reported validation evidence is produced in computational settings; physical-domain experimentation is described only as future work.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: At present, The AI Scientist conducts computational experiments only.

diagnostics.consequence: Empirical adequacy claims are bounded by computational fidelity; no channel can test physical-world validity.

diagnostics.statement: The literature-grounded novelty process is described mechanistically but no reliability study of its novelty judgments is reported.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: queries external academic databases to identify knowledge gaps and avoid rediscovering existing work

diagnostics.consequence: Undetected prior-art misses at ideation propagate into every downstream stage without any later novelty check.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: an LLM-based evaluator assesses all leaf nodes and selects the most promising one to serve as the root for the next stage

success_check.observable: Rate at which the stage-transition evaluator prunes leaf nodes that later outperform the retained root
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Compared against random and metric-only leaf selection baselines on held-out runs
success_check.evidence_needed: Counterfactual continuation of pruned branches with reported outcome metrics

exact_quotes.exact_quote: a higher F1 score compared with inter-human group agreement (0.62 versus 0.49)
reflection rounds to refine proposals based on the literature search results

success_check.observable: Novelty-specific validation of the ideation filter separate from the manuscript reviewer benchmark
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Independent expert prior-art adjudication on a sampled set of accepted proposals
success_check.evidence_needed: Property-specific external validation report for the novelty target

exact_quotes.exact_quote: numerical scores for soundness, presentation and contribution
Reviewers were informed that some of the submissions were AI-generated but not which ones, ensuring a blind process.

success_check.observable: Documented routing of reviewer and workshop-review outputs into node selection or stage transition
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: At least one implemented path where a review score alters a subsequent search action
success_check.evidence_needed: Execution traces showing review-conditioned changes in tree-search decisions

exact_quotes.exact_quote: This process resulted in three complete manuscripts being selected for submission.

success_check.observable: Explicit statement of which decisions are human-selected versus machine-generated at each stage
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Every reported autonomy claim scoped to generation and execution, excluding selection
success_check.evidence_needed: Stage-by-stage authority table with counts of filtered and retained outputs

exact_quotes.exact_quote: a preliminary accept or reject decision

success_check.observable: Whether the reviewer's accept/reject output is used as an advisory diagnostic or as an acceptance gate
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Documented policy distinguishing advisory scores from any binding gate
success_check.evidence_needed: Specification of the decision role plus traces of how the output was consumed

exact_quotes.exact_quote: In future work, this same playbook could be applied to other scientific domains where one can automatically conduct experiments (or have humans conduct them) and collect data from them

success_check.observable: Claims about physical-domain validity in the reporting
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: No validity claim beyond computational experiments until a physical channel is exercised
success_check.evidence_needed: Explicit scoping statement bounding conclusions to computational fidelity
